description_long: This object consists of a group of wooden storage crates, each constructed from planks with visible wood grain and seams. The crates vary in size and shape, with some featuring open tops and others partially closed with removable or slotted lids. Several crates have cut-out handholds on the sides, making them easier to lift and carry. The wood appears aged and slightly worn, suggesting frequent use or outdoor exposure. The overall arrangement is stacked and slightly irregular, indicating these boxes are often moved or rearranged in practical urban settings such as warehouses, garages, or alleys.
description: A group of stacked, weathered wooden storage crates with open and closed tops, some featuring handholds and removable lids.
description_view_0: This view shows the crates stacked at various angles, highlighting the open tops and the handholds on the side of the upper crates.
description_view_1: From this angle, the front faces of the crates are visible, with one lid resting on top and the open interior of a large crate clearly seen.
description_view_2: This perspective emphasizes the open crate in the foreground and the slotted handholds on the side crates, with more detail on the crate interiors.
description_view_3: The rear and side panels of the crates are visible, showing the solid wood construction and the stacked arrangement.
